title: Jump Challenge
description: A 2D side-scrolling platformer arcade game with procedural platform generation, AABB collision physics, gravity-based kinematics, difficulty scaling, and localStorage high-score persistence.
author: Girish Madarkar
features: Gravity kinematics: per-frame vy += 0.55 with instantaneous negative impulse jump (vy = -11) locked to grounded state only
AABB landing detection: horizontal overlap check + descending-base threshold (playerBot ∈ [pTop, pTop + vy + 6]) snaps player to platform surface
Procedural platform queue: platforms spawned at randomized x-offsets with currentGap() widening per difficulty level, culled behind cameraX - 200
Camera tracking: lerp-based (0.08) smooth follow with 0.3W leading offset for lookahead
Difficulty curves: speed BASE_SPEED + (level-1)×0.35, gap PLAT_MIN_GAP + (level-1)×4px, 20 levels max, incremented every 300 score
localStorage persistence: jcBest key read on boot, written on game-over if score exceeds stored record
Canvas resize handler: re-maps canvas buffer to parent container dimensions via getBoundingClientRect on orientation change
Multi-modal input: mousedown, touchstart (with preventDefault), and Space keydown all trigger doJump() if player is grounded
